Title: Define LangGraph State Schema
UserStory: As a developer, I want one shared LangGraph task state schema so every node reads and writes the same structured state.
DetailedDescription: Create the canonical task state object for the new LangGraph pipeline. The schema should include smell metadata, task classification, refactor plan, edit output, verification output, critique result, artifact paths, retry counters, and final task status. This story is intentionally first because it unblocks all graph, planner, and verifier integrations while remaining small and well bounded.
StartCondition: Project kickoff and agreement on core fields
FinishCondition: Schema file is checked in and referenced by pipeline code
AcceptanceCriteria: One documented state schema exists; all required fields for classify, plan, edit, verify, critique, and finalize are defined; schema is understandable by all three teammates
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/state.py — TaskState TypedDict with all lifecycle fields, make_initial_state() helper, status constants. JSON schema: schemas/langgraph_task_state.schema.json

Title: Define Actionability Classification Schema
UserStory: As the system, I want a structured actionability schema so each smell can be labeled actionable, non_actionable, or needs_review before editing starts.
DetailedDescription: Define the classification contract with required fields such as label, rationale, confidence, smell-specific caveats, and whether a no-op is acceptable. This prevents the system from treating ambiguous detector findings as valid refactor targets and gives verification clearer rules.
StartCondition: Project kickoff and smell label agreement
FinishCondition: Classification schema is checked in and referenced by prompts or nodes
AcceptanceCriteria: Classifier labels are explicit; rationale is mandatory; schema distinguishes actionable from intentional or false-positive patterns
Deliverable: Actionability schema JSON or typed model
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/schemas/actionability.py — ActionabilityResult TypedDict, ACTIONABLE/NON_ACTIONABLE/NEEDS_REVIEW constants, validate_actionability_result() with no_op constraint. JSON schema: schemas/actionability_result.schema.json

Title: Define Refactor Plan Schema
UserStory: As the system, I want a structured refactor plan schema so editing is gated by an explicit React-aware plan rather than freeform generation.
DetailedDescription: Define the JSON or typed contract for the planner output. Include tactic name, files to edit, risk level, invariants, expected smell resolution, and reasons to abort. This story is foundational because both prompt design and verification logic depend on consistent plan fields.
StartCondition: Project kickoff and initial smell list agreed
FinishCondition: Plan schema is checked in and available to planner and verifier
AcceptanceCriteria: Planner schema includes tactic, file scope, invariants, risks, and expected outcome; invalid plans can be rejected before edit stage
Deliverable: Plan schema JSON or typed model
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/schemas/plan.py — RefactorPlan TypedDict, risk level constants, validate_refactor_plan() with allowed-file scope enforcement. JSON schema: schemas/refactor_plan.schema.json

Title: Define Benchmark Dataset Schema
UserStory: As a researcher, I want a benchmark dataset schema so the team can evaluate refactor quality on a consistent labeled task set.
DetailedDescription: Create the data model for benchmark tasks. Include smell type, target files, line range, actionable label, expected tactic, difficulty, validation commands, and reviewer notes. This can begin immediately and does not block the runtime graph, which makes it good parallel work.
StartCondition: Project kickoff and agreement on benchmark goals
FinishCondition: Benchmark schema is documented and stored in repo
AcceptanceCriteria: Dataset schema supports demo tasks now and future expansion later; fields are sufficient for scoring and human review

Title: Build Verification Scaffold
UserStory: As the system, I want a verification scaffold for parse, build, typecheck, and lint so unsafe edits are caught automatically.
DetailedDescription: Create the verification layer interface and node scaffolding, even if some checks are no-op per repo. The key goal is to define how verification results are captured, how failures are reported, and how later checks plug in. Starting this early lets the team integrate real validation without blocking on planner implementation.
StartCondition: State schema is clear enough to record verification results
FinishCondition: Verification node scaffold runs and writes structured results
AcceptanceCriteria: Verification supports structured result capture for AST parse, build, typecheck, lint, and custom smell checks even if some checks are initially stubbed

Title: Implement Minimal LangGraph Pipeline
UserStory: As a developer, I want a minimal LangGraph pipeline with placeholder nodes so the current linear runner can be replaced incrementally.
DetailedDescription: Implement the first working LangGraph graph with nodes for classify, plan, edit, verify, critique, and finalize. The nodes can initially be thin wrappers around existing behavior, but task flow should now be graph-driven. This is the core runtime migration story and the main platform anchor for the demo.
StartCondition: State schema is approved
FinishCondition: A task can traverse the graph from start to finish with placeholder or existing logic
AcceptanceCriteria: Graph executes node sequence deterministically; node transitions are explicit; tasks can run one by one through LangGraph without relying on the old sequential runner
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/graph.py (StateGraph wiring), langgraph_pipeline/nodes/ (classify/plan/edit/verify stubs + real finalize node), langgraph_pipeline/runner.py (run_task and run_tasks entry points). Conditional edge routes non_actionable/needs_review directly to finalize.

Title: Implement Actionability Classifier Node
UserStory: As the system, I want a classifier node that labels each smell before planning so the pipeline does not waste effort on bad or ambiguous tasks.
DetailedDescription: Build the first model-backed classifier node using the actionability schema. It should process smell artifacts and output structured labels plus rationale. For demo quality, focus especially on distinguishing truly fixable smells from intentional React patterns.
StartCondition: Actionability schema exists
FinishCondition: Classifier writes structured output per task
AcceptanceCriteria: Classifier outputs valid schema-conforming JSON; actionable vs non_actionable is explicit; rationale is human-readable and stored as an artifact
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/nodes/classify.py — real OpenAI gpt-4o-mini call using function calling for structured ActionabilityResult output. Hard pre-check gate: smell types not in KNOWN_ACTIONABLE_SMELL_TYPES are rejected without LLM call. System prompt includes full smell-to-refactoring table. Tested on 14 real Supabase smells across 7 smell types.

Title: Implement Refactor Planner Node
UserStory: As the system, I want a planner node that creates a bounded refactor plan only for actionable smells so code edits are controlled and reviewable.
DetailedDescription: Build the planner node that consumes the smell, context, and actionability output. It should emit a plan using the plan schema. It must refuse overly broad or unclear plans and should identify a tactic plus invariants for the verifier and critic to enforce.
StartCondition: Plan schema exists and LangGraph skeleton runs
FinishCondition: Planner writes valid plan artifacts for actionable tasks only
AcceptanceCriteria: Planner produces valid plans; non-actionable tasks do not proceed to edit; each plan specifies tactic, file scope, invariants, and risk

Title: Create Curated Demo Benchmark Set
UserStory: As a researcher, I want a curated benchmark of 25 to 40 labeled React smell tasks so the team can test the system on trustworthy examples before the demo.
DetailedDescription: Build the first benchmark slice manually and carefully. Choose only smell categories that are likely to produce compelling refactors in two weeks. Each task should be labeled for actionability and expected tactic. This story is critical because it keeps the demo grounded in high-confidence examples rather than random detector output.
StartCondition: Benchmark schema exists
FinishCondition: Initial benchmark files are complete and reviewed
AcceptanceCriteria: At least 25 labeled tasks exist; each has smell type, actionability label, expected tactic, and reviewer notes; demo subset is clearly identified

Title: Preserve Existing Artifact Flow in LangGraph
UserStory: As a researcher, I want every LangGraph node to write artifacts into the current run structure so debugging and comparison remain easy.
DetailedDescription: Wire LangGraph execution to the existing run directories and task artifacts. The new graph should feel compatible with the current research workflow. This story matters because the demo will be much stronger if the audience can inspect planner outputs, verifier results, and critique decisions in familiar folders.
StartCondition: Minimal graph runs successfully
FinishCondition: Each major node writes artifacts into run and task folders
AcceptanceCriteria: Existing run layout is preserved or cleanly extended; artifacts exist for classification, plan, edit, verify, critique, and final outcome

Title: Build Initial React Tactic Library
UserStory: As a maintainer, I want a small React tactic library so the planner and editor work from known transformation patterns instead of generic refactoring instructions.
DetailedDescription: Define and document the initial set of tactics for the demo smell classes. For each tactic, capture preconditions, common edit shape, expected invariants, and known risks. This story spreads well across the team because it needs both React insight and verification awareness.
StartCondition: Plan schema exists and demo smell list is agreed
FinishCondition: Initial tactic library is available to planner and prompts
AcceptanceCriteria: At least 3 to 5 tactics are defined; each includes preconditions, invariants, and risk notes; tactics are specific enough for prompts and verification to use
ImplementationNotes: Implemented: langgraph_pipeline/tactics.py — 23 tactics covering all 19 smell types from the reference table. Each tactic has: name, display_name, applies_to_smells, preconditions, edit_shape, invariants, risks (low/medium/high), abort_if. Lookup helpers: get_tactic(), get_tactics_for_smell(), tactic_names_for_smell(), all_covered_smell_types().

Title: Reject No-Op Success for Actionable Tasks
UserStory: As the system, I want actionable tasks with no edits or empty outcomes to fail by default so the demo cannot falsely claim success.
DetailedDescription: Implement the rule that a no-op is only acceptable when the classifier marked the smell non_actionable or needs_review with clear rationale. This directly addresses the failure mode seen in the current pipeline where preserving behavior alone can incorrectly pass a refactor attempt.
StartCondition: Actionability labels exist and verification scaffold can record rule outcomes
FinishCondition: No-op results are evaluated correctly based on actionability
AcceptanceCriteria: Actionable tasks with no edits fail verification or critique; non-actionable tasks can pass with explicit rationale; behavior is captured in artifacts
Deliverable: No-op rejection rule in verification or critique

Title: Make Edit Prompts Tactic-Guided
UserStory: As the system, I want the edit prompt to reference the chosen tactic and invariants so the model performs a bounded React-specific transformation.
DetailedDescription: Update the edit stage so prompts are derived from plan outputs and tactic metadata instead of generic smell instructions. This reduces drift, improves repeatability, and gives the verifier clearer expectations. It also balances workload by letting platform and evaluation work inform prompt shape.
StartCondition: Planner and tactic library are usable
FinishCondition: Edit prompt generation consumes tactic and invariant data
AcceptanceCriteria: Prompts explicitly reference tactic, allowed scope, invariants, and expected smell resolution; freeform broad rewrites are discouraged

Title: Add Smell-Resolution Checks for Supported Smells
UserStory: As the system, I want smell-resolution checks for the supported demo smells so we can confirm the refactor actually addressed the target issue.
DetailedDescription: Implement targeted resolution checks for the initial supported smell types. These checks can be heuristic at first, but they should tie directly to the tactic and expected outcome. This is one of the highest-value demo stories because it shows the system validates success semantically, not just syntactically.
StartCondition: Benchmark and tactic definitions are stable enough
FinishCondition: Verification can mark supported smells resolved or unresolved
AcceptanceCriteria: Supported smell types have explicit resolution logic; results are recorded per task; unresolved actionable smells fail or are flagged

Title: Implement Resume and Retry From Node
UserStory: As a developer, I want to resume a task from a named LangGraph node so failed demo tasks can be rerun quickly without restarting the pipeline.
DetailedDescription: Add resumability and targeted retry controls for task execution. This is not as demo-critical as classification and verification, but it greatly reduces team friction during debugging and live rehearsal. It can be developed in parallel with smell-resolution work.
StartCondition: Minimal graph is stable
FinishCondition: A failed or paused task can resume from a chosen node
AcceptanceCriteria: CLI supports resume-from-node or retry-from-node for a task; state is restored correctly; duplicate artifact confusion is avoided

Title: Show Node-Level CLI Progress
UserStory: As a presenter, I want node-level CLI progress so the audience can understand what the system is doing during the demo.
DetailedDescription: Add readable progress output for graph execution. Show which node is running, which nodes passed or failed, and the final decision. This story makes the system easier to explain and helps your team debug in rehearsals.
StartCondition: Minimal graph is stable
FinishCondition: CLI prints understandable node-level progress and final status
AcceptanceCriteria: Task execution output is readable; node names and statuses are visible; failures are easy to locate from terminal output

Title: Integrate End-to-End Demo Flow
UserStory: As the team, we want one end-to-end LangGraph flow over a curated set of demo tasks so we can present a convincing working system in two weeks.
DetailedDescription: Integrate classification, planning, tactic-guided editing, verification, and artifact reporting into one stable demo run. Use only the curated benchmark subset for the first polished demo. This is the main integration milestone and should be protected from scope creep.
StartCondition: Core graph, planner, prompts, and verification stories are complete enough
FinishCondition: A curated demo run completes end to end with inspectable outputs
AcceptanceCriteria: At least 5 to 10 curated tasks can run end to end; outputs are understandable; the system correctly fixes, skips, or fails tasks with evidence

Title: Generate Demo Report and Evidence Pack
UserStory: As a presenter, I want an automatically generated report showing fixed, skipped, failed, and reviewed tasks so stakeholders can understand the demo outcome quickly.
DetailedDescription: Generate a concise but evidence-rich summary for the curated demo run. Include task counts, classification results, chosen tactics, verification outcomes, and before/after diffs or references. This story makes the demo legible and credible.
StartCondition: Artifacts are stable and demo run exists
FinishCondition: A readable report is generated automatically after the run
AcceptanceCriteria: Report includes totals, task statuses, reasons for skips or failures, tactic names, verification outcomes, and links or paths to key artifacts

Title: Expand Benchmark Beyond Demo Slice
UserStory: As a researcher, I want a larger benchmark so the month-end system is evaluated on more than the polished demo tasks.
DetailedDescription: Scale the benchmark beyond the hand-picked demo subset. Add more tasks, more smell categories, and clearer reviewer guidance. This is important for honest evaluation after the demo but should not distract from the first two-week milestone.
StartCondition: Demo benchmark and workflow are stable
FinishCondition: Expanded benchmark is available and versioned
AcceptanceCriteria: Benchmark size and coverage increase meaningfully; labels remain reviewed and usable for future experiments

Title: Improve Context Gathering Using Plan Needs
UserStory: As the system, I want context gathering to better match planner needs so the model sees the most relevant local code for each tactic.
DetailedDescription: Refine the context gathering stage to include files and snippets that matter for the selected tactic rather than relying only on nearby imports and siblings. This can increase planning and edit quality, but it should come after the demo-critical pipeline is working.
StartCondition: Demo workflow is stable enough to evaluate context gaps
FinishCondition: Context selection improves on benchmark tasks
AcceptanceCriteria: Context gathering is measurably more relevant for supported tactics; artifacts show why files were included

Title: Add Human Review Gate for Needs-Review Tasks
UserStory: As the team, we want a clean human review gate for needs_review tasks so ambiguous smells do not silently disappear or fail unclearly.
DetailedDescription: Implement a clear workflow path for tasks classified as needs_review. These tasks should be separated in reports and optionally skipped from auto-editing. This increases trust in the system once the main automated path is stable.
StartCondition: Classification and reporting are stable
FinishCondition: Needs-review tasks are routed clearly and reported separately
AcceptanceCriteria: Needs-review tasks do not enter auto-edit blindly; reports show them as a distinct category with rationale

Title: Measure Patch Quality and Failure Modes
UserStory: As the team, we want patch-quality metrics so we can identify whether failures come from planning, editing, verification, or detector quality.
DetailedDescription: Add metrics for diff size, scope violations, plan validity, smell-resolution rate, no-op rate, and verification pass rate. This story helps the team decide where to invest next after the demo and whether any future fine-tuning is justified.
StartCondition: Stable month-end pipeline exists
FinishCondition: Quality metrics are generated across benchmark runs
AcceptanceCriteria: Metrics make failure sources legible and support future roadmap decisions
